question: How do I connect to CPCE_Student Wi-Fi on Android?
answer:
Based on the strongest evidence, - Open **Settings** > **Wi-Fi** under **Connections** or **Network & Internet**.
- Turn on Wi-Fi and select **CPCE_Student**.
- Configure network properties:
  - EAP method: **PEAP**
  - Phase 2 authentication: **MSCHAPV2**
  - CA certificate: **Use system certificates**
  - Domain: **wifi.cpce-polyu.edu.hk**
  - Identity: **Your StudentID**
  - Password: **Your portal password**
- Tap **Connect**. [1]\n\nSources:\n[1] How do I connect to CPCE_Student Wi-Fi on an Android phone? (ITU_FAQ_002_wi_fi_connection, current, effective 2026-02-01)\n[2] How do I connect to CPCE_Student Wi-Fi on an iPhone? (ITU_FAQ_001_wi_fi_connection, current, effective 2026-02-01)\n[3] Cannot browse websites when connected to Student Wi-Fi (ITU_FAQ_004_wi_fi_connection, current, effective 2026-02-01)

question: How do I connect to CPCE_Student Wi-Fi on iPhone?
answer:
Based on the strongest evidence, - Go to **Settings** > **Wi-Fi**.
- Select **CPCE_Student**.
- Press **Accept** if the certificate shows "wifi.cpce-polyu.edu.hk" and "Go Daddy Secure Certificate Authority - G2".
- You should now be connected. [1]

question: What should I do if Eduroam does not connect?
answer:
Based on the strongest evidence, - Double-check credentials: **StudentID@cpce.hk** & your portal password.
- Verify certificate: "wifi.cpce-polyu.edu.hk" and "Go Daddy Secure Certificate Authority - G2".
- Go to Settings > Wi-Fi. Tap the Actions Available button, then turn Limit IP Address Tracking off.
- Restart your device if issues persist. [1]

question: How do I check my print quota?
answer:
Based on the strongest evidence, To check your printing quota, you can follow these steps:

Method 1: Check via mobile app
1. Download and install the CPCE Mobile App on your smartphone.
2. Click on “Print Quotas Balance”.
3. Your current print quota will be displayed on the screen.

Method 2: Check at the printing terminal
1. Go to the printing terminal located at South Tower 1/F, North Tower 7/F, or South Tower 9/F.
2. Log in to the printing terminal using your CPCE account credentials or tap your student card.
3. Your current print quota will be displayed on the screen.

Method 3: Check at the iPrint Kiosk
1. Go to the iPrint Kiosk
2. Tap your student card.
3. Your current print quota will be displayed on the screen. [1]\n\nSources:\n[1] How can I check my print quota? (ITU_FAQ_019_printing_services, current, effective 2026-02-01)\n[2] Print tips (ITU_FAQ_026_printing_services, current, effective 2026-02-01)\n[3] How can I increase my print quota? (ITU_FAQ_020_printing_services, current, effective 2026-02-01)

question: How can I increase my print quota?
answer:
Based on the strongest evidence, - Students top-up their print quota by Octopus card at iPrint Kiosk located at the express printing area and near SCC. (Remaining quotas will be carried forward to next academic year. Print balance is non-refundable and will be cleared for graduated or quitted students) 
- HKD 1 = 10 print quota [1]

[1] How can I increase my print quota? (ITU_FAQ_020_printing_services, current, effective 2026-02-01)

question: How much does colour A4 printing cost?
answer:
Based on the strongest evidence, - Colour
  - 15 Print Quotas Per A4 Page
  - 30 Print Quotas Per A3 Page

- Black & White
  - 2 Print Quotas Per A4 Page
  - 4 Print Quotas Per A3 Page

- If printing or copying black and white in colour printer, it will cost the same as colour (15 Print Quotas Per A4 Page, 30 Print Quotas Per A3 Page) [1]\n\nSources:\n[1] Service charge of printing service (Printing/Photocopying) (ITU_FAQ_021_printing_services, current, effective 2026-02-01)\n[2] How to print (ITU_FAQ_025_printing_services, current, effective 2026-02-01)\n[3] Print tips (ITU_FAQ_026_printing_services, current, effective 2026-02-01)

question: How do I print using Wi-Fi printing?
answer:
Based on the strongest evidence, 1. Visit:
   - [Wifi Printing website link](https://wkwifiprint.cpce-polyu.edu.hk/)
2. Upload your document. (Tips: if using Google Drive, please download the file to your device before uploading to the system)
3. Select a printer (**B/W** or **Colour**). (Remember to use the correct printer to release the file)
4. Wait for the status change to "sent to printer".
5. Release the print job at the designated KM Printer. [1]\n\nSources:\n[1] How do I print from my device using Wi-Fi (Wi-Fi Printing)? (ITU_FAQ_023_printing_services, current, effective 2026-02-01)\n[2] What is the Wi-Fi password (ITU_FAQ_005_wi_fi_connection, current, effective 2026-02-01)\n[3] Cannot browse websites when connected to Student Wi-Fi (ITU_FAQ_004_wi_fi_connection, current, effective 2026-02-01)

question: How do I release a print job from a black and white printer?
answer:
Based on the strongest evidence, - Touch **Release** (on top-right)
- Tap your **Student Card** on the card reader for authorization.
- Touch **Release** (in the middle left)
- Select the documents to print from the list.
- Press **Start** to begin printing.
- Press **Logout** after collecting your documents. [1]\n\nSources:\n[1] How do I release a print job from B/W printers? (ITU_FAQ_027_printing_services, current, effective 2026-02-01)\n[2] How do I release a print job from Colour printers? question: Can students get NETID and NetPassword?
answer:
Based on the strongest evidence, - Students are not provided with NETID. If it is for accessing Po Yue-kong Library online resources, HKCC/SPEED students can only access Po Yue-kong Library online resources via PC inside Po Yue-kong Library. When login PC inside PYK Library, please use CPCE_ID@student.cpce.hk where CPCE_ID is your CPCE student no., and the password will be the same as your CPCE portal password. [1]

Sources:
[1] How to have NETID and NetPassword? (ITU_FAQ_039_password_management, current, effective 2026-02-01)
[2] connect to VPN (ITU_FAQ_007_wi_fi_connection, current, effective 2026-02-01)
[3] How can I increase my print quota? (ITU_FAQ_020_printing_services, current, effective 2026-02-01)

question: How do I borrow a power bank?
answer:
Based on the strongest evidence, - **Loan Locations:** Power Bank Vending Machine (S2/F)
- **Hours:** 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM
- **Penalty for Late Returns:** Starts at **200 print quotas** for the first day, please refer to ITU website or the notice at the IT service counter. [Equipment overdue table](https://itu.cpce-polyu.edu.hk/it-policies/student-notebook-powerbank-loan-service)
- **Return Location:** IT service counter on South Tower, 2/F
- **Details:** [Power Bank Loan page](https://studenthkccpolyuedu.sharepoint.com/sites/PowerBankLoanpage/) [1]

[1] How do I borrow a power bank (battery)? (ITU_FAQ_041_equipment_loan_services_power_bank_macbo, current, effective 2026-02-01)

[3] How to borrow a notebook/MacBook? (ITU_FAQ_042_equipment_loan_services_power_bank_macbo, current, effective 2026-02-01)

question: What are the SCC opening hours?
answer:
Based on the strongest evidence, - Each express printing area (South Tower 1/F, North Tower 7/F, or South Tower 9/F has Printers and PCs with internet access (Time control software applied that logs off PC after 15 minutes, you are required to save your work within the time limit).
- Express Printing Area is available during campus opening hours. Students are advised to check the opening hours of different floors, especially on Sundays and Public holidays, as some floors are closed at that time. [1]
